title: paraprasophyllum readii
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum readii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting any part of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Overexposure to the plant's oils can lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can cause skin burns and blisters.
